steps.title: Fastest path: organize the side labels first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Recognize that angles N and P are complementary
steps.content: Since angle M is a right angle (90°), and the sum of angles in a triangle is 180°, we have M + N + P = 180°. Substituting M = 90°: 90° + N + P = 180°, which simplifies to N + P = 90°. This means N and P are complementary angles.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity for complementary angles
steps.content: When two angles sum to 90°, their trigonometric functions are cofunctions of each other: $\cos(N) = \sin(P)$ and $\sin(N) = \cos(P)$. Given that $\cos(N) = \frac{30}{78}$, we can immediately write $\sin(P) = \frac{30}{78}$.

steps.title: Solve for cos(P) using the Pythagorean identity
steps.content:
Applying the Pythagorean identity $\sin^2(P) + \cos^2(P) = 1$:
$$\left(\frac{30}{78}\right)^2 + \cos^2(P) = 1$$
$$\frac{900}{6084} + \cos^2(P) = 1$$
$$\cos^2(P) = 1 - \frac{900}{6084} = \frac{6084 - 900}{6084} = \frac{5184}{6084}$$
$$\cos(P) = \sqrt{\frac{5184}{6084}} = \frac{\sqrt{5184}}{\sqrt{6084}} = \frac{72}{78} = \frac{12}{13}$$
The answer is 12/13.
